description: GOLDEN PROVENANCE registry (docket SB.2, 019f77117376). Records whether each golden was blessed ONCE by an INDEPENDENT authority (an independent Gerber viewer and/or kicad-cli DRC on the round-tripped board). A golden with blessed=false, or with no entry here, is UNTRUSTED: usable by the geometry-diff harness as a DRIFT PIN, but NEVER as a correctness oracle. See tests/oracle/provenance.py and golden/HOW_TO_BLESS.md.

spike-gerber-v1.notes: REGENERATED 2026-07-19, PENDING owner gerbv re-bless (Stage 2 pad-bug closure, docket 019f761fe518). The 4 SMD lands changed from the SYNTHETIC hardcoded 1.2x1.3mm to the REAL 0805 land 1.0x1.45mm (KiCad R/C_0805_2012Metric), now SOURCED by RESOLVING vendored seed-lib footprints (Resistor_SMD:R_0805_2012Metric, Capacitor_SMD:C_0805_2012Metric, TestPoint:TH_TestPoint) instead of a hardcoded generate.py constant — this is the whole point of Stage 2 (geometry from library resolution, not placeholders). MINIMAL DELTA vs the prior owner-blessed golden: ONLY the 4 SMD lands (F_Cu 1.2x1.3->1.0x1.45; F_Mask 1.4x1.5->1.2x1.65). The U1 O1.6 TH pad, O0.8/O0.4 drills, O3.2 NPTH mount, VCC/GND traces, and 40x30 Edge_Cuts are UNCHANGED and were previously blessed. RE-BLESS SCOPE: the owner need only re-confirm the 4 real 1.0x1.45mm SMD lands in gerbv. ORACLE SCOPE (Option A): the correctness test asserts only copper/mask/drill/edge (fab-critical) via GeometryDiff.excluding_layers('F_SilkS') — silk is EXCLUDED because the emitter generates courtyards procedurally, differently from this golden's hand-drawn ones; real silk correctness is tracked separately (silk-text/image 019f77fd6d69; coverage audit 019f77fd9c6c). ANTI-CIRCULARITY preserved: this golden is regenerated by the INDEPENDENT generate.py (gerber-writer lib); the emitter under test is pcb_worker.gerber (pygerber) — different code paths. While blessed=false the correctness-oracle test SKIPS-WITH-REASON; once the owner sets blessed=true it becomes a LIVE copper-scoped assertion and pad bug 019f7736b236 CLOSES.

emitter-snapshot-v1.notes: DRIFT-PIN ONLY — captured from the emitter under test (pcb_worker.gerber.build_gerbers via scripts/capture_emitter_golden.py). Inherently circular: it can detect FUTURE drift in the emitter but can NEVER attest correctness. blessed stays false PERMANENTLY by design; do NOT bless this entry. The correctness reference is spike-gerber-v1.

cam-excellon-ipc356-v1.method: OWNER bless 2026-07-18. DRILLS+SLOT: full independent VISUAL confirmation in gerbv (/opt/homebrew/bin/gerbv) overlaid on board frame + F_Cu — both plated holes (Ø0.8 @ U1, Ø0.4 @ via) concentric inside their copper pads, routed slot ~4x1mm @ lower-right, bare Ø3.2 NPTH mount @ lower-left. IPC-356: confirmed as SYMBOLIC net-membership match to the design source board.yaml (VCC={U1.1,R1.2}, GND={R1.1,C1.1}) + test-point locations == already-blessed pad centers. NOT independently visualized as scalable geometry.
cam-excellon-ipc356-v1.notes: BLESSED WITH CAVEAT (owner, 2026-07-18, docket 019f777e9206). DRILLS+SLOT: high confidence — fully visually verified in gerbv. IPC-356: MODERATE confidence WITH DOCUMENTED CAVEAT — the owner could verify it only as symbolic 'pure math' net-membership (matches board.yaml source) + locations (== already-blessed pads), NOT as scalable visible geometry, because NO IPC-356 -> visual verification tool exists yet (tracked in 019f77aa012e; that tool would render net-colored test points spatially so a netlist can be blessed the way drills were). Owner: 'I believe, with some uncertainty, that it is correct.' Emitted by the RATIFIED gerbonara coupon (spikes/cam/emit_golden.py -> gerbonara_coupon.build()). Satisfies CAM ratification condition 1. Contents: PTH = Ø0.8mm plated @ (30.0,15.0) [U1 TH] + Ø0.4mm plated @ (20.0,10.0) [via] + a Ø1.0mm plated routed SLOT (34.0,5.0)->(38.0,5.0); NPTH = Ø3.2mm bare @ (5.0,5.0) [mount]; IPC-356 = 4 records: U1.1 VCC through-hole Ø0.8 plated @ (30.0,15.0) access-both, R1.2 VCC SMD @ (10.95,10.0) top, R1.1 GND SMD @ (9.05,10.0) top, C1.1 GND SMD @ (9.05,20.0) top. ANTI-CIRCULARITY CAVEAT: emitted by gerbonara and the Stage-5 PRODUCTION emitter will ALSO be gerbonara-based (different code path — pcb_worker over the ResolvedBoard IR), so this golden catches INTEGRATION / driving divergence (wrong params/geometry from the IR) but NOT gerbonara LIBRARY bugs (those are tracked in 019f7773257 and caught by the owner's INDEPENDENT gerbv bless of drills+slot + IPC-356 record review). CORRECTNESS CONSUMER: the Stage-5 production Excellon/slots/IPC-356 emitter (019f761fefae) — which does NOT exist yet, so no correctness comparison is possible today. Until BOTH the owner blesses (blessed=true) AND that consumer exists, the correctness-oracle test skips-with-reason; a coupon-vs-its-own-output check is a drift/reproducibility pin ONLY, never a correctness claim. Do NOT self-bless — see golden/HOW_TO_BLESS_EXCELLON.md; flag open Excellon-header bug 019f7720928d during the drill check.
